title: Time-resolved ARPES on cuprates: Tracking the low-energy electrodynamics in the time domain
journal: Journal of Electron Spectroscopy and Related Phenomena

paper_analysis.summary: This paper reviews and analyzes time-resolved ARPES (TR-ARPES) on Bi-based cuprate superconductors, focusing on how low-energy electronic dynamics evolve after near-IR pumping. The main findings are that nodal coherent spectral weight suppression is well described by Fermi-liquid temperature broadening, while near-nodal superconducting-gap filling is non-thermal and linked to pair-breaking bosons that transiently enhance the pair-breaking scattering rate.
measurement_types: Fermi surface mapping / iso-energy contour mapping

figures.description: Log-scale schematic/plot of transient electronic temperature versus pump-probe delay, illustrating two relaxation regimes after optical excitation and a cartoon of energy flow from hot electrons to phonons.
Nodal equilibrium and transient EDCs plus their difference, showing transient spectral-weight loss and gain near the Fermi level.
Transient evolution of the near-nodal superconducting gap for two pump fluences, with symmetrized EDCs and phenomenological fits.
Nonequilibrium antinodal EDCs at different delays after pump excitation, highlighting excess states beyond simple heating and transient in-gap spectral weight.
Time dependence of photoemission intensity at an O-2p band binding energy for two pump energies, compared with antinodal in-gap-state growth and peak broadening.
Schematic of electron-electron versus electron-boson scattering contributions and a plot of fast decay time versus pump fluence above the Fermi level.
Diagram of the contributions to TR-ARPES intensity: spectral function, matrix elements, Fermi-Dirac distribution, and scattering/relaxation terms.
Schematic density of states for doped cuprates showing lower/upper Hubbard bands, charge-transfer gap, and near-IR excitation pathways.
Equilibrium Fermi-surface map of optimally doped Bi2212 before pumping.
Differential iso-energy contour map 0.5 ps after pumping, showing intensity redistribution above the Fermi level.
Momentum-integrated nodal EDCs before and after pumping for Bi2201-OD24 with Fermi-Dirac fits.
Transient electronic temperature extracted from Fermi-edge fits for two pump fluences.
Nodal EDCs at kF for Bi2212-OP91 and Bi2201-OD24 at low and high electronic temperature, comparing TR-ARPES and equilibrium ARPES.
Relative variation of nodal coherent spectral weight versus electronic temperature for multiple Bi-based compounds, compared with Fermi-liquid simulations.
Temperature dependence of the spectral function at ω=0 and kF, compared with Fermi-liquid and marginal-Fermi-liquid fits.
Experimental differential momentum-integrated nodal EDC map versus delay for Bi2201-OD24.
Simulated differential nodal EDC map generated from the Fermi-liquid model using the measured electronic temperature.
Transient electronic temperature versus delay with double-exponential fit.
Population and depletion dynamics extracted from the differential nodal maps, with fits.
Off-nodal TR-ARPES spectra of Bi2212-OP91 before and after pumping, showing superconducting-gap filling.
Transient tomographic density of states at selected delays, showing transfer of spectral weight into the gap region.
Symmetrized EDCs at kF for selected delays with fits to the phenomenological self-energy model.
Time dependence of gap amplitude, single-particle scattering rate, pair-breaking scattering rate, and electronic temperature.
Symmetrized off-nodal EDCs for Bi2212-OP91 with fits using the extended self-energy model.
Transient evolution of gap and pair-breaking scattering rate for Bi2212-OP91, with electronic temperature and a BCS-like thermal-gap reference.
Same as Fig9a1 but for overdoped Bi2212-OD60.
Same as Fig9b1 but for Bi2212-OD60.
Same as Fig9a1 but for Bi2201-OD24.
Same as Fig9b1 but for Bi2201-OD24.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The paper reports TR-ARPES measurements on Bi-based cuprate single crystals, including equilibrium ARPES and pump-probe ARPES maps, EDCs, Fermi-surface maps, and off-nodal spectra. It also mentions pump fluences, probe photon energies, pump photon energies, and temperature-dependent measurements on Bi2212 and Bi2201 samples.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: Many plotted results are processed or derived from the raw ARPES spectra: symmetrized EDCs, momentum-integrated EDCs, Fermi-edge fits to extract electronic temperature, differential maps (pump-on minus pump-off), TDOS, coherent spectral-weight integrals, self-energy fits, and simulated curves/maps from Fermi-liquid or phenomenological models.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: No explicit data availability statement, repository link, or file-format description is present in the provided text. The paper mainly describes the measurements and analysis methods, and notes that some figures are adapted from prior references and that new experimental data are presented.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are described only at the level of experimental observables: TR-ARPES spectra, EDCs, momentum-resolved intensity maps, Fermi-surface maps, and equilibrium ARPES spectra collected on Bi2212 and Bi2201 under various pump-probe delays and temperatures. No file format, directory structure, or instrument data format is given in the text.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include symmetrized EDCs, momentum-integrated EDCs, differential intensity maps, extracted electronic temperatures, coherent spectral-weight integrals, TDOS curves, and fitted parameters such as gap amplitude, single-particle scattering rate, and pair-breaking scattering rate. The paper also presents model-generated simulations based on Fermi-liquid and phenomenological self-energy equations.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific data are clearly implied: each main figure is built from extracted spectra, integrated intensities, or fitted parameters. However, the text does not state that separate source-data files are provided for figures, nor does it mention supplementary source-data archives.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repositories are mentioned in the provided text. The analysis is described mathematically through equations and fitting procedures, but implementation details are not provided.
dataset_characterization.replot_reason: The paper provides enough methodological detail to understand how plots were generated, but it does not provide explicit source-data files, raw data files, or scripts in the text. Therefore the dataset is not directly replot-ready from the paper alone.
dataset_characterization.notes: The paper mixes raw TR-ARPES measurements with extensive derived quantities and model fits. Several figures are explicitly described as adapted from prior references, so not all plotted data are newly generated in this paper. The most important semantic distinction is that many central results are not raw spectra but processed quantities extracted from spectra via symmetrization, integration, deconvolution, and fitting.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The paper discusses direct TR-ARPES measurements, equilibrium ARPES spectra, Fermi-surface maps, and delay-dependent spectra on Bi-based cuprates.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The paper presents many extracted and fitted quantities such as symmetrized EDCs, differential maps, TDOS, coherent spectral weight, and fitted self-energy parameters, which are figure-level processed data.
classification_prior.both_evidence: The text implies both direct measurements and processed figure data, but does not explicitly mention a source-data archive.
data_availability_section_relevant: simulated map has been generated via Eq. 2
no explicit data availability statement in provided text
classification_prior.review_notes: Be careful not to classify all plotted curves as raw data: many are derived from ARPES spectra through symmetrization, integration, subtraction, deconvolution, or model fitting. Also note that some figures are adapted from earlier publications, so the paper is partly a review and partly a presentation of new data.
